issues.title: Test suite: 14 order-dependent failures in full local run (singleton leakage), invisible to CI

labels.name: testing
labels.description: Issues related to test suite and testing infrastructure

issues.body: Surfaced by #582/#589: once the stale-load-error mask was removed, a FULL local GUT run (`python scripts/run_godot_tests.py`) shows ~14 failures in test_game_manager.gd (13) + test_turn_manager.gd (1) — difficulty money (Easy asserts 367500, gets 225000), a $25k salary deduction, and signal/turn-flow timing. **CI's Unit Tests gate passes** (main + #589 green), so these are order-dependent, not caught in CI's mode. Almost certainly singleton/state leakage between tests (GameConfig.difficulty etc.) rather than game bugs — difficulty modifiers verifiably work in-game (#585). Fix: proper per-test isolation (reset GameConfig/singletons in before_each/after_each); then confirm whether any are genuine regressions vs pure test artifacts.

issues.title: CI test gate is a no-op: Godot unit/integration/smoke suites report green while running ZERO tests
issues.body:
## Severity: high (latent). The Godot test gate has been passing without executing tests.

Found during a 2026-07-13 test-infra audit (Fable Admin session). Empirically verified from live CI logs, not inferred.

## Evidence (CI run 29213378045, "Unit Tests" job)

```
23:26:28.655  ERROR: Some GUT class_names have not been imported...
23:26:28.998  [TEST] Running tests in res://tests/unit
23:26:28.999  [PASS] Tests in res://tests/unit passed! CHECKED   <- 1 ms later
23:26:29.001  [SUCCESS] All tests passed!
              ##[warning] No files were found... test-results-*.xml
```

A pass 1 ms after "Running tests", no results file written. Nothing ran. Same in Integration; Smoke "passes" pointing at `tests/smoke/` **which does not exist**.

## Root cause — two independent, compounding bugs

1. **CI never runs `godot --headless --import`.** On a fresh checkout GUT's own `class_name`s aren't in the class cache, so GUT hits `error_if_not_all_classes_imported()` → `quit(0)` in `gut_cmdln.gd:_init`, **before parsing args or running a test**. (`.github/workflows/godot-tests.yml` has no import step; the only `import` token is a comment.)
2. **The runner trusts the bare exit code.** `scripts/run_godot_tests.py:84` treats `returncode == 0` as PASS — no results-file parse, no floor on tests-executed. So `quit(0)`-with-nothing reads as success.

Underlying flaw: an exit code that is 0 for "all passed", "ran nothing", AND "quit early" is not a gate.

### Bonus bug (the `-gjunitxml_file` false-RED that started this)
`run_godot_tests.py:73` sends `-gjunitxml_file=` but vendored GUT 9.5.0 registers `-gjunit_xml_file` (underscores, `gut_cli.gd:150`); GUT `quit(1)`s on the unknown arg. This only bites a box whose class cache is warm (editor opened once) so GUT reaches arg-parsing — which is why an agent saw a local exit-1 while CI stayed green. The "CI's Linux GUT accepts it" explanation was wrong: CI never reaches the arg.

## Sibling issues (neighborhood scan)
- **#590 (GUT hides parse-failed test files)** is the same disease; its extreme case (all tests hidden) is live now. Same fix (results-count floor) closes both.
- **Syntax gate under-detects:** runner + workflow grep for `Cannot load source code` / `GDScript error` but MISS Godot's actual `Parse error` / `Failed to load script` strings — real parse failures pass the syntax gate.
- **Orphaned tests** in `godot/tests/*.gd` (root) are never scanned (runner walks only `unit/`/`integration/`/`smoke/`).
- **Hardcoded `"test-seed"` boot** (`main_ui.gd:400`) — no test guards a fixed production seed.

## Remediation plan (~1 hr core), ordered
1. Add `godot --headless --import` before every GUT invocation (runner + direct integration call). *(root of the false green)*
2. Stop trusting the exit code: require the JUnit file to exist and parse `tests > 0, failures == 0`; treat early-`quit(0)`/no-results as failure. *(closes #590, the no-op, and the empty-suite hole together)*
3. Fix arg spelling `-gjunitxml_file` -> `-gjunit_xml_file` (`run_godot_tests.py:73`).
4. Broaden syntax-error patterns to include `Parse error` and `Failed to load script` (runner + `godot-tests.yml:44`).
5. Stretch: real `tests/smoke/` boot->new-game->end-turn test (or make a missing smoke dir hard-fail); reclaim orphaned root tests; remove/guard the `test-seed` boot.

## Handling notes (from Pip)
- **This gets PRIMO attention and effort when we take it on** — not a drive-by. It's the safety net under everything else.
- **Expect it to light up red.** Turning the gate on will surface previously-hidden real failures. We WANT to root-cause each of those, and for each, **scan the local neighborhood for sibling issues and adjacent code failures** — same discipline that found this. Budget for that follow-up, don't just make it green again.
- **Shelved deliberately** until after the pending workshop implementation + QA round (fixing it now would flip in-flight lane PRs from false-green to red mid-landing).

## Not-alarm note
The gate being hollow means it was **permissive, not blocking** — no bad merge was gated *out*. The workshop lanes (L0/L7/L9/L10/L8/L6) were each verified locally by their finishing agents running the real suite directly with the import pass (e.g. L8 334/334, L9/L10 325/325), so merged code is genuinely tested; it's the automated gate that's worthless.

issues.title: Build lane L1 · Turn engine: plan-months, day ticks, response windows (ADR-0009)
issues.body:
**Authority:** ADR-0009 (+ ADR-0012 for window menu semantics). Work order: `docs/game-design/WORKSHOP_2_BUILD_LANES.md` §L1. **CRITICAL PATH** — L2/L3/L4/L5 queue behind this.

Scope:
- Month = the turn: plan phase (staff/founder-hour allocation, reserve, queued actions) → day-by-day resolution (speed control + auto-pause-on-window) → month review screen
- Guard rule: **no routine decision on the day tick**
- Response-window plumbing: costed menu [HANDLE-reserve / HANDLE-cannibalize / DEFER / IGNORE] (ledger side wired in L4)
- Reserve crisp (evaporates); delete banking
- Strategic actions get durations (fundraising money lands later)
- Re-denominate: salaries monthly, loans %/month placeholder, event pacing per month
- Replay schema bump: record (event_id, window, choice, payment_source); batch DQ-6 schedule provenance into same version
- Display badge = exact death date; score internals unchanged

Acceptance: deterministic replay round-trip incl. responses; constant-policy bots re-baselined (gate G1 prerequisite).

**Session kickoff prompt:** "Read docs/game-design/WORKSHOP_2_BUILD_LANES.md lane L1 and ADR-0009/ADR-0012. Also read the architecture-review findings comment on this issue before touching turn_manager. Implement L1; anything not covered by the ADRs goes to WORKSHOP_2_BACKLOG.md as a DQ, not into code."

⚠️ Wait for the arch-review comment (incoming) before starting.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

issues.title: Build lane L10 · main_ui decomposition: extract-before set + let-die map
issues.body:
**Authority:** `docs/TECH_DEBT_REGISTER.md` §L10 + `WORKSHOP_2_BUILD_LANES.md` §L10. **Start now — parallel-safe.** main_ui.gd is 3,786 lines of imperatively-built dialogs; follow the existing child-widget mount pattern (`research_quality_selector.gd` et al).

Extract NOW (turn-model-independent), in order:
1. **`event_dialog.gd`** (:2975-3223) — FIRST; L1's response windows reuse exactly this presenter
2. `ledger_screen.gd` (:1857-1985) — zero turn/AP coupling
3. `employee_panel.gd` (:3304-3582) — becomes L2's per-person assignment surface
4. `submenu_chrome.gd` (:1088-1195) — shared dialog positioning/styling

Sever while extracting: direct state writes `main_ui.gd:535` (queued_actions) and `:1524-1526` (hire queue/candidate pool); delete the 8× duplicated affordability pre-checks (trust `select_action`'s error signal).

**LET-DIE — do not extract, polish, or fix:** command/queue/reserve-AP/commit-plan zone (:397-544), queued-actions viz (:2866-2974), `_calculate_queued_costs`, AP-affordability half of the action bar (:909-928). The L1/L2 plan screen replaces these; new screens are NEW scenes speaking only to GameManager's API.

Domain dialogs (hiring/fundraising/financing/publicity/strategic/travel/operations) extract DURING L2/L3 when their AP triggers die — not now.

**Session kickoff prompt:** "Read docs/TECH_DEBT_REGISTER.md §L10. Extract the four before-set modules in order; behavior-preserving; never touch the LET-DIE list."

issues.title: Build lane L9 · Balance surface + data externalization (defs→JSON, Balance autoload)
issues.body:
**Authority:** `docs/TECH_DEBT_REGISTER.md` §L9 + `WORKSHOP_2_BUILD_LANES.md` §L9. **Run with/after L0 (#620), BEFORE L1 (#612).** Behavior-preserving: current values become defaults; replays must verify.

Audit findings driving this lane:
- **No balance-config surface exists** — every tunable is an inline literal across 10+ core files; `game_config.gd` is player settings only; `data/events/balancing/*.json` are well-structured but loaded by NOTHING.
- **~1,900 lines of dict literals in core engine files**: `events.gd` is ~1,100/1,483 lines data (`get_all_events` + `get_risk_events`); `actions.gd` definitions (:101-500) + risk-contribution table (:846-969) are pure data. The event merge pipeline (`events.gd:874-896`) already supports external sources.

Scope:
1. `Balance` autoload (Resource/JSON-backed, mirror `scenario_loader` pattern) — the sweep's tuning surface (DQ-8/ADR-0013 numbers become file-swaps)
2. Seam first: `get_months_per_turn()` + salary `/260` + `TURNS_PER_WEEK` + week→turn `×5` conversions route through the L0 Clock reading Balance
3. Externalize: events → `data/events/core_events.json`+`risk_events.json`; actions → `data/actions/*.json` (+`risk_contributions.json`); template inline `format_money` as placeholders
4. Shared `ResourceAccessor` (replaces 3× resource-name match in events.gd/event_service.gd)
5. Wire `difficulty.json` as multipliers over Balance, or delete the dormant trio

Payoff: L1 rewrites ~600 lines of engine instead of 2,665 of engine-plus-data; every ADR-0013 number becomes sweep-tunable without code edits.

**Session kickoff prompt:** "Read docs/TECH_DEBT_REGISTER.md §L9 + WORKSHOP_2_BUILD_LANES.md §L9. Behavior-preserving; replay-verify after each step; migration order as written."

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

issues.title: Architecture smell: two GameManager instances (scene-local + autoload); 'ready' prints twice
issues.body: Found via #600/#607: main.tscn instances a scene-local GameManager (which main_ui runs), while a bareword GameManager autoload singleton ALSO exists and is never populated ('[GameManager] ready' prints twice). #607 routed the dev overlays to the live scene node, but the dead autoload lingering is a latent smell that caused the 'no game state' bug and could cause others. Consolidate to one source of truth. Also parked from #607: F3 Game State/Performance tabs still render BBCode in plain Labels (need RichTextLabel); dev-mode pokes can desync the verification log (gate/log before real use).

issues.title: Build lane L8 · Achievements skeleton (observer-only)
issues.body:
**Authority:** `WORKSHOP_2_BUILD_LANES.md` §L8 + backlog DQ-17. **Start anytime — small, parallel-safe.**

Scope:
- **Observer-only contract:** achievements are read-only listeners on run events; they NEVER write back to sim or score (ADR-0002 anti-sink rule — recognition, no in-run reward)
- v1 set: year marks (survived 2022/2027/2032/2037 — free since badge = death date), first-time-X local profile flags
- "Fastest X this season" deferred until leaderboards (EE-4)
- Candidate register: WORKSHOP_2_BACKLOG DQ-17 ([ACHIEVEMENT] tags accumulate there)

**Session kickoff prompt:** "Read WORKSHOP_2_BUILD_LANES.md lane L8. Build the listener infra + v1 set; keep the observer-only contract absolute."

issues.title: Build lane L5 · Cost-of-debt pricing engine + financing instruments (ADR-0013)
issues.body:
**Authority:** ADR-0013. Work order: `WORKSHOP_2_BUILD_LANES.md` §L5. **Depends on L4; numbers gated on L6 (EE-8).**

Scope:
- One engine for loans/defers/funding-with-strings: cost = f(org_type [major], counterparty [major], typed_rep, purpose_hype, ledger_load [minor])
- Purpose-tagged fundraising (hype prices the purpose); NO internal earmarked budgets
- Instruments: equity (single dilution scalar v1), board seats (stub "options-curtailed" flag pending DQ-7), agenda narrowing
- Tuning target / gate **G3**: philanthropy-only viable-but-slow; trade-heavy openings faster, billing late-game optionality

**Session kickoff prompt:** "Read WORKSHOP_2_BUILD_LANES.md lane L5 + ADR-0013 + ADR-0003. Do not tune numbers until EE-8 death attribution (L6/#617) is merged."

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

issues.title: Build lane L4 · Event taxonomy + ledger intake (ADR-0012)
issues.body:
**Authority:** ADR-0012. Work order: `WORKSHOP_2_BUILD_LANES.md` §L4. **Depends on L1; parallel with L2.** Absorbs BL-1..3 (the DEFER menu IS the ledger's player-facing wiring).

Scope:
- Event classes: un-snoozable (borders/emergencies/legal/story — no DEFER) · deferrable (mints ledger entry) · standing offers (expire to no-engage, NO entry) · no-action-correct
- Called-due cascade: unpaid staff damage → departures → work abandoned → rep collapse → credit lockout → funding starvation → rival ahead → doom spike (no ledger death screen — cascade must be legible)
- Fundraising lead time; infinite to-do list is diegetic
- Event content schema: class, expiry N, response-expected, `where`, carrying-cost hook

Acceptance: contributes to sweep gate **G1** — greedy policies produce >0 ledger-attributed deaths.

**Session kickoff prompt:** "Read WORKSHOP_2_BUILD_LANES.md lane L4 + ADR-0012 + ADR-0003. Implement on top of the L1 window plumbing."

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

issues.title: Build lane L3 · Adoption, papers, conferences (ADR-0010, ADR-0014)
issues.body:
**Authority:** ADR-0010, ADR-0014. Work order: `WORKSHOP_2_BUILD_LANES.md` §L3. **Depends on L2.**

Scope:
- Safety research splits: internal hardening (own-lab, private) vs publishable agendas (world/rival doom, adoption-routed)
- Pipeline: research → paper → socialization → adoption → doom bends; unadopted world-facing research is a PDF
- Reputation per-person + per-org, typed (safety vs finance); typed-attention events
- Conferences as seed-schedule entries (annual majors announced ~9 months out; exclusive gatherings gated by contacts/safety-rep); founder ≫ delegate attendance; yields = adoption acceleration, faster rep, contacts-as-receivables, SA presence boost
- Location minimal: `where` on events; American HQ default; overseas basing = cheaper hires + stubbed influence hooks

Acceptance: sweep gate **G2** — publish-and-socialize beats basement-spam. Balance check: rival/world doom share must dominate the integral.

**Session kickoff prompt:** "Read WORKSHOP_2_BUILD_LANES.md lane L3 + ADR-0010 + ADR-0014. Implement on top of L1+L2."

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

issues.title: Build lane L2 · Effort economy: founder hours, staff lanes, managers (ADR-0011)
issues.body:
**Authority:** ADR-0011. Work order: `WORKSHOP_2_BUILD_LANES.md` §L2. **Depends on L1.**

Scope:
- Delete global AP pool; founder-hours menu (doors / approvals / audits / reserve)
- Per-person staff assignment to multi-month workstreams from a backlog; unassigned staff self-direct within-lane
- Workstream milestone ticks (working papers, blog posts → partial rep/SA)
- Managers: absorb team's interrupt classes; plan-screen compression (housed members, team report); Celine's-law report channel stub; agenda riders via existing ledger staff-rider factory
- Ops/admin reduce founder-price of routine actions
- Researcher model: lane × appetite × quirk-rider; roster in WORLD_AND_LORE (retention promises = ledger entries)

**Session kickoff prompt:** "Read WORKSHOP_2_BUILD_LANES.md lane L2 + ADR-0011 + the staff-archetype roster in WORLD_AND_LORE.md. Implement L2 on top of the L1 turn engine."

issues.title: Popups: fanfare needs a dimming backdrop (events leak under it); bug-report 'thanks' Submit still clickable
issues.body: Playtest 85ad582: (1) The Strategic-Moves **fanfare lets background events animate underneath it** — add a dimming/black-fade backdrop (~0.5s, Civ-II-negotiation style) that blocks + draws attention. (2) Bug-report (N): after submit it flashes 'thanks' but the **Submit button stays clickable** in the thanks state — disable/hide it.

issues.title: Navigation audit + universal principle (ESC/back consistency, mirrored toggle keys, unreachable Employee screen)
issues.body: Playtest 85ad582: nav inconsistencies. Employee screen has no back-to-main except a button — **ESC there opens the game menu** instead of returning to main UI. **No native path to the Employee screen** from main UI (needs an affordance/icon). Establish a PRINCIPLE for consistent coverage sweeps: mirrored toggle keys (a key that opens a panel closes it) + consistent ESC/back (returns to previous/main, not game menu, unless top-level). Audit all screens/overlays against it.

issues.title: Ledger UX: layout wrong/cramped, L-to-close, critical-due popup, distinct warning colour
issues.body: Playtest 85ad582 (screenshot in dev-blog images): ledger dialog layout wrong — entry text cramped top-left + truncated ('due 3t @25%/t' cut off), dialog mostly empty. (1) Make content **fill the dialog / bigger**, uniquely styled — it *looks* draggable but isn't; Pip's pref = bigger inner content (not draggable). (2) **L should close** the ledger too (mirror toggle). (3) **CRITICAL balance-due** should raise a blocking popup/acknowledge-action, not just a log line. (4) Ledger warnings in the event log need a **distinct colour (red)**, not normal-event green.

issues.title: Dev overlays (DEV MODE + F3) can't read live game state; 29 runtime errors; nudge/advance-turn wiring
issues.body: Playtest 85ad582: DEV MODE (backslash) and F3 both report **'no active game state'** during a live game, so risk-pools + rival-labs sections are empty. F3 also shows **29 runtime errors** (all 'color white refcounted ...') + a perf tab (RAM/FPS) but no risk pools. DEV MODE resource +/- **nudge buttons may not be wired** to change state (verify). **Advance-turn** gives no visible feedback (works? overlay may cover the UI). Also: **backslash should CLOSE** dev mode (toggle). Design guard needed: dev-mode state pokes should NOT corrupt the verification/event log.
